key_points: The lesson 'Kathmandu' is written by Vikram Seth and describes his visit to Nepal’s capital city
The author visits two important religious places: Pashupatinath temple and Boudhanath stupa
Pashupatinath temple shows the busy and chaotic side of religious rituals
Boudhanath stupa represents calmness and spiritual peace
The flute seller’s music symbolizes harmony among different cultures
characters: Narrator – Vikram Seth, who describes his travel experience and observations
Flute seller – A calm street vendor who sells bamboo flutes by playing music
Temple priests and devotees – People performing rituals and worship at Pashupatinath temple
themes: Cultural and religious diversity in Kathmandu
Contrast between chaos and peace in spiritual places
Music as a universal form of expression
literary_devices: Imagery – Vivid descriptions of temples, streets, sounds and rituals
Simile – The flutes are compared to the quills of a porcupine
Contrast – The difference between Pashupatinath temple and Boudhanath stupa
exam_tips: Explain the contrast between the two religious places
Mention the role of the flute seller in the lesson
Focus on cultural diversity and the importance of music
